title: Public Calculation API Route Tasks
principles: Keep public API routes calculator, fact, rule and graph driven.
Keep jurisdiction, tax year and rule-pack options as schema-backed calculator context.
Reuse canonical schemas, descriptors, tagged errors and report types from owning packages.
Return Effect Schema decode issues with field paths and descriptor-backed help.
Keep apps/api thin; calculation contracts and handlers stay in packages/http-api.
Use Effect Layers, CalculationEngine and canonical rule-pack Layers for execution.
Keep OpenAPI generated from Effect HTTP API definitions.

globalVerification.commitPolicy: Commit only after each coherent implementation slice passes verification, Changeset review and API contract review.
requiredBeforeFinalPR: bun run changeset or explicit no-changeset rationale
OpenAPI JSON includes the implemented public calculation routes
API smoke checks for metadata routes and at least one successful calculation
API smoke check for schema-guided validation error help
Architecture audit against docs/architecture/api-and-sdk.md, facts.md, rules-and-parameters.md, calculators.md and graph-trace-ledgers.md
evidenceRequired: Changeset path and release-train impact, or no-changeset rationale
API changelog update for public route changes

tasks.title: Create the calculator catalog and shared API schemas
tasks.goal: Add the schema-backed catalog and API boundary types needed for calculator, fact, rule and graph routes without implementing public calculation execution yet.
tasks.implementationPrompt: You are implementing exactly one task from the WhatTax spec/task list. Before editing, read docs/product-specs/public-calculation-api-routes.md, this task object, relevant current files, and the relevant docs in docs/architecture/*. Identify the owning package for every schema, type, ID, service, layer and tagged error you touch. MUST use Effect-native primitives when they fit: Data, Schema, Array, Chunk, HashSet, HashMap, Match, Context, Layer, Config, Service, Record, Result, Exit, Bun, Platform, Command and ManagedRuntime. Reuse canonical schema-derived types, branded IDs, tagged errors, service contracts and constructors from the owning package. Do not create mirrored DTOs, local duplicate types, local duplicate schemas or transport-only shape copies when an owning schema or API/SDK contract already exists. Keep one-off Effect error handling and transformations inline at the callsite. Browser/runtime code must consume browser-safe API/SDK exports instead of importing server-only internals. Package-facing changes MUST add or update a Changeset with bun run changeset using a user-facing changelog summary. Run mandatory verification gates, report the Changeset path and release-train impact or no-Changeset rationale, report changed files and residual risks, and stop after this task for parent review.
outputs: Schema-backed calculator ID and context schemas
Schema-backed public error envelope for schema decode and unsupported calculator/context failures
Initial calculator catalog entries for au.pay.take-home, au.pay.withholdings and au.income-tax.annual
Catalog entries reference canonical fact descriptors, rule descriptors, report schemas and rule-pack Layers
Package README note for public calculation API schema ownership
mandatoryVerification: Search changed files for mirrored canonical fields such as local id: string definitions where an owning branded ID/schema exists
Search changed files for Object.entries, Object.values, manual _tag objects, unsafe casts and non-null assertions
completionCriteria: Catalog and API boundary schemas are Effect Schema-backed
Canonical descriptors and report schemas are imported from owning packages
No calculation route is exposed yet unless its handler is complete
Parent agent verified canonical schema/type/id reuse
Changeset covers @whattax/http-api and any package whose public exports changed

completionCriteria: Metadata routes are generated through Effect HTTP API groups
OpenAPI JSON contains every implemented route
Graph route returns canonical graph diagnostics shape
Facts and rules routes support calculator, jurisdiction and taxYear filters
API changelog documents the new public metadata surface

tasks.title: Implement schema-guided calculation execution
tasks.goal: Expose POST /api/v1/calculators/:calculatorId/calculate with canonical scenario decoding, rule-pack Layer selection, CalculationEngine execution and schema-guided errors.

outputs: Successful calculation responses for au.pay.take-home, au.pay.withholdings and au.income-tax.annual
Schema-guided decode error responses with missing field paths and descriptor-backed help
Client exports updated for browser-safe route consumption
API changelog entry for public calculation execution
mandatoryVerification: Run apps/api locally and smoke check successful au.pay.take-home calculation
Run apps/api locally and smoke check successful au.income-tax.annual calculation
Run apps/api locally and smoke check a schema error for missing grossPay.period with help=errors
Confirm calculation handlers use CalculationEngine and canonical rule-pack/scenario Layers
Confirm request handlers do not create runtimes or import app runtime modules
completionCriteria: Calculation route returns schema-backed report, diagnostics, trace and ledger values where relevant
Schema errors are machine-readable and include help metadata when requested
Rule-pack dispatch stays Layer-based and calculator-driven
withholdings calculation reuses the take-home calculation path rather than creating a divergent engine
API changelog documents public calculation execution
